competitor_patterns: Top-ranking content is extremely long-form (16,000–24,000 words), covering multiple products, use cases, and buyer profiles in a single comprehensive roundup — far exceeding our current 2,000–3,100 word posts
Competitors consistently use comparison tables (has_table: true) to allow scannable side-by-side evaluation of weight, lens type, price, and fit — a format completely absent from all our recent posts
Every top-3 result combines FAQs, expert reviews, and structured H2s organized by rider type (MTB, road, women, budget) rather than by product feature — they answer intent segments, not specs

Bikerumor and OutdoorGearLab lead with 'Tested' or 'Ranked' credibility signals in their meta titles and H1s, establishing hands-on authority immediately — our titles lead with product name or feature

Competitors update content frequently (Bikerumor: '3 days ago', OutdoorGearLab: 'Jan 2026', Rouvy: 'Apr 2026') and signal freshness explicitly in snippets — our posts lack visible date signals in titles or snippets
our_gaps: Zero comparison tables across all recent posts — competitors use tables as a core UX and SEO element to target 'best X for Y' queries and improve dwell time; we have none

Our posts are feature-centric (UV protection, anti-fog, weight) rather than rider-persona-centric — top competitors structure content around 'best for road', 'best for MTB', 'best for women', 'best budget', which maps directly to how searchers query
Word counts are 2,000–3,100 words vs competitor benchmarks of 8,600–24,000 words — we are not comprehensive enough to compete for head or mid-tail keywords on topical authority alone

No product comparison content exists — we never directly benchmark StradaPro against Oakley Sutro Lite, Tifosi Rail, or Julbo Density, which are the named products searchers encounter in top results and then search to compare

Our meta titles lead with product name + feature (e.g. 'UV Protection Cycling Glasses 2026 | Velluto StradaPro') rather than benefit-first or test-credibility framing — this reduces CTR against competitors who use 'Tested', 'Ranked', 'How to Choose' signals
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Demonsun ranks #1, we have the exact feature (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione click-in system) but no dedicated page targeting this query
lightweight cycling sunglasses 25g — no competitor owns this ultra-specific weight query; our 25g frame is a concrete differentiator that maps to a real purchase decision trigger
best road cycling glasses anti fog — combines two high-intent modifiers (road + anti-fog) that align directly with StradaPro's built-in system; not covered as a standalone topic
cycling glasses 30 day trial — a near-zero competition long-tail query where our unique risk-free trial policy could rank uncontested and convert high-intent hesitant buyers

best cycling sunglasses for small faces — OutdoorGearLab and Rouvy reference fit for different face sizes; our adjustable nose pads solve this directly and we have a German-language post about women's fit but no English equivalent targeting this query
next_post_guidelines: Write at minimum 5,000 words for any post targeting a head or mid-tail keyword ('best cycling glasses', 'road cycling glasses review', 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses') — include multiple H2 sections organized by rider persona (road, gravel, MTB, commute, women, budget) not just by feature
Include at least one HTML comparison table per post — minimum columns: Product, Weight, Lens System, Anti-Fog, Price, Trial Policy — always position StradaPro as the benchmark row at the top

Frame every meta title with a credibility or benefit signal first: use formats like 'Best Cycling Glasses for [Persona] 2026 — Tested' or '[Feature] Cycling Glasses: [Specific Claim]' — keep under 60 characters and never lead with brand name

Add a 'How We Tested' or 'Real Ride Notes' section to every review-style post — even a 150-word first-person trail/road test section dramatically increases E-E-A-T signals that Google's algorithm and AI overviews weight heavily

Target the 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' keyword cluster with a dedicated post built around the lens-swap workflow — include step-by-step instructions with subheadings, a table comparing VellutoPuro vs VellutoVisione VLT and use cases, and a direct comparison to Demon Sunglasses' polycarbonate lens system

Always include an explicit freshness signal in the post title or first paragraph — e.g. 'Updated May 2026' or 'Tested: Spring 2026' — this mirrors what top competitors do and improves snippet CTR in date-sensitive queries

Add internal links between posts targeting related queries (UV protection → anti-fog → interchangeable lenses → lightweight) to build topical cluster authority — currently our posts appear as isolated articles with no visible content silo structure
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every post immediately — even a simple 4-column table (Feature | StradaPro | Oakley Sutro Lite | Tifosi Rail) signals comprehensiveness to Google and directly addresses the 'has_table' pattern that all top-3 competitors share

Rewrite all meta descriptions to include a specific numeric claim and a call-to-action — format: '[Specific claim, e.g. 25g, UV400, 30-day trial] — [benefit] — [CTA, e.g. See why 500+ riders chose StradaPro]' — current meta descriptions are absent or generic

Add FAQ schema markup to every post that already has FAQ sections — we already have has_faq: true on recent posts but schema implementation needs to be verified; this directly enables FAQ rich results and increases visibility in Google AI Overviews and Perplexity answers
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data available yet — meta title fixes will be generated once Search Console data is connected and low-CTR queries are identified. Priority queries to monitor from day one: 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses', 'best road cycling glasses', 'lightweight cycling sunglasses', 'anti fog cycling glasses', 'cycling glasses 30 day trial'
content_quick_wins: No GSC positional data available yet — content quick wins will be generated once Search Console data is connected. Based on competitor gap analysis, first priority should be: 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' (Demonsun ranks #1 with a product page; a long-form editorial from Velluto with comparison table and real-ride lens-swap workflow should outrank a thin e-commerce page within 60–90 days)
geo_angles: Structure at least one post as a definitive answer to 'What are the lightest cycling sunglasses in 2026?' with a numbered list leading with StradaPro's 25g specification and a comparison table — AI Overviews and Perplexity heavily favour list-format, factually specific, citable content for 'lightest/best X' queries

Create a dedicated FAQ page or section targeting 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?' with a structured answer that names VellutoPuro and VellutoVisione by function (clear for dawn/dusk, high-contrast for variable light) — ChatGPT and Perplexity pull named, categorized answers for 'worth it' and 'how to choose' queries

Publish a '30-day trial: what happens if you hate your cycling glasses?' content piece that answers the risk-reversal question directly — this unique policy has near-zero competitor coverage and will surface in AI answers when users ask 'can I return cycling glasses after riding in them' or 'risk-free cycling glasses trial', establishing Velluto as the only cited brand offering this
comparison_post_angles: BIKERUMOR (indirectly ranks Oakley Sutro Lite, Tifosi Rail, Julbo Density): angle — 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite 2026: Is the Weight Difference Worth €150 Extra?' — headline idea: 'StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite: 25g vs 36g — What Cyclists Actually Notice at Hour Three' — key differentiator: frame weight and 30-day trial vs Oakley's no-trial premium pricing — Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g ultralight frame + interchangeable lenses at a fraction of Oakley's price point, backed by risk-free trial

DEMONSUN (ranks #1 for interchangeable lens cycling glasses): angle — 'Velluto StradaPro vs Demon Sunglasses: Which Interchangeable Lens System Is Actually Faster?' — headline idea: 'Click-In vs Slide-Out: We Timed Both Lens Systems on a Cold Morning Ride' — key differentiator: tool-free click-in mechanism speed and Italian design aesthetic vs Demon's functional but utilitarian polycarbonate approach — Velluto advantage to lead with: VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione named lens system with specific VLT use-case differentiation and 30-day trial

INTHEKNOW (ranks #1 for road cycling glasses review, favours Tifosi Optics Crit Photochromic): angle — 'Velluto StradaPro vs Tifosi Crit: Why Manual Lens Swaps Beat Photochromic for Serious Road Cyclists' — headline idea: 'Photochromic vs Interchangeable Lenses for Road Cycling: The Case Against Slow-Reacting Tints' — key differentiator: instant manual lens swap in variable conditions vs photochromic lag time — Velluto advantage to lead with: VellutoVisione high-contrast lens optimised for specific light conditions rather than a compromise auto-tint, plus 25g weight advantage over Tifosi's heavier photochromic frames
